qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Attacker-controlled stdout can spoof NodeJS.call results
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets attacker-controlled stdout spoofing against the post-fix UID-based protocol, while the advisory is the pre-fix stale-response race where timed-out responses from earlier calls were misdelivered to later callers because no request-response correlation ID existed.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's timeout-driven stale-response race. The only final qualifying finding is a different stdout-protocol injection issue that abuses attacker-controlled logging and predictable UIDs after the advisory fix added request-response correlation.
evidence: Issue #100 describes the advisory bug as timed-out call results being picked up by later calls because late Node.js responses remained in the GenServer mailbox and there was no request ID to correlate replies.
PR #105 fixes that advisory by adding a monotonic per-worker UID to each request and making `get_response` discard protocol frames whose UID does not match the in-flight call.

The session finding explicitly depends on that UID-based protocol: its report cites `uid_counter` and `uid:json` framing, and its PoC forges `__elixirnodejs__UOSBsDUP6bp9IF5__0:[true,"forged"]` on stdout.

The finding PoC is pinned to commit `9cd32e631e82a722ff0e61b588a2813461a53dcd`, the head commit from PR #105, so it exercises a different flaw in the fixed protocol rather than the advisory's missing-correlation bug.
